diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Political Communication from ancient persuasion to digital algorithms. Key milestones: ancient rhetoric (Aristotle's Rhetoric, 4th cent. BCE); the printing press and political pamphlets (16th‑18th c.); the party press era (19th c.); the rise of mass media (FDR's fireside chats, 1933; Kennedy‑Nixon TV debates, 1960); the professionalization of campaigns (political consultants, 1970s‑80s); 24‑hour cable news (CNN, 1980); the internet and online campaigning (Howard Dean blog, 2003; Obama's social media, 2008); the fake news and post‑truth era (2016); and contemporary developments in AI, deepfakes, and algorithmic microtargeting (2020s). The timeline illustrates the ongoing transformation of how politics is communicated.
